metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state (SQLite/PostgreSQL databases for series metadata, episode tracking, download history) and exposes CRUD operations on business entities (series, episodes, download clients, indexers). It has both read and write endpoints with entity lifecycle management.
metadata.context: TV-series PVR for usenet and BitTorrent users (*arr suite).

classification_consistency_check.reason: Score 1.88 yields V5 Needs Work but 14 High findings force V6 Not Ready. This application is a desktop/self-hosted PVR with no cloud infrastructure — the high finding count reflects absence of cloud-native capabilities rather than critical failures. Surface-gating applied correctly (has_deployed_workload=false prevents false evaluation of deployment-specific capabilities, but INF questions legitimately score 1 because no cloud infrastructure exists).

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC exists — all infrastructure would need to be created from scratch
top_gaps.impact: Blocks all cloud deployment; no reproducible infrastructure

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No cloud compute defined; application is a self-hosted desktop/server binary
top_gaps.impact: Cannot scale, no managed patching, no elastic capacity

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or cloud audit logging
top_gaps.impact: No compliance visibility, no forensic capability

top_gaps.title: Monolith vs Microservices
top_gaps.gap: Tightly-coupled monolith with shared database and in-process event bus
top_gaps.impact: Cannot independently scale components; all-or-nothing deployments

top_gaps.title: Deployment Strategy
top_gaps.gap: No staged deployment; self-update mechanism replaces binary in-place
top_gaps.impact: High risk of failed updates with no automated rollback

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists (README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, external wiki). APP-Q5 >= 2.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Index OpenAPI specs (V3/V5), README, CONTRIBUTING guide, and wiki content to answer developer questions about the Sonarr API and configuration.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: External wiki content needs crawling and indexing. OpenAPI specs are already machine-readable.

quick_agent_wins.name: API-aware Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: OpenAPI V3 and V5 specs exist at src/Sonarr.Api.V3/openapi.json and src/Sonarr.Api.V5/openapi.json. APP-Q5 = 3.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Agent discovers and invokes Sonarr API endpoints as tools — managing series, triggering searches, checking download status via natural language.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: API requires authentication (API key), agent needs credential configuration.

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (GitHub Actions with build, test, deploy stages). INF-Q11 = 3.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Agent triggers builds, checks test results, monitors deployment status, and manages releases through existing GitHub Actions pipeline.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Needs GitHub API access configured for the agent.

decomposition_strategy.recommended_approach: Conditional/Adaptive (containerize first, then selective extraction via Strangler Fig)

approaches.name: Strangler Fig (Parallel Track)
approaches.rationale: Existing layered architecture provides identifiable extraction boundaries

approaches.name: Conditional / Adaptive
approaches.rationale: Application already detects Docker and disables self-update

approaches.name: Big-Bang Rewrite
approaches.rationale: Monolith is functional and well-structured internally

findings.title: No managed compute infrastructure defined
findings.description: No cloud compute infrastructure is defined. The application is distributed as self-contained binaries for 10 runtime identifiers and is designed to run as a self-hosted server process on user-managed hardware.
findings.gap: All compute is self-managed with no managed container orchestration or serverless adoption.
findings.recommendation: Containerize the .NET 10 application and deploy to Amazon EKS. Create a modern Dockerfile using the .NET 10 ASP.NET runtime image.

findings.title: Databases are entirely self-managed
findings.description: The application uses SQLite (embedded, default) and PostgreSQL (optional, user-provisioned). Both are entirely self-managed with no managed database services.
findings.gap: All databases are self-managed with no managed services, no automated failover, no managed backups.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL for the PostgreSQL deployment path. Existing Npgsql driver and FluentMigrator migrations are compatible.

findings.title: No network security controls defined
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or network segmentation configuration exists. The application binds to a local port on the host machine with no cloud-native network segmentation.
findings.gap: No network security controls defined. Application exposes API directly on host network.
findings.recommendation: Define a VPC with private subnets for application and database tiers. Use security groups with least-privilege rules. Place API behind ALB or API Gateway.

findings.title: No API gateway or load balancer
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront configured. Application serves HTTP directly from ASP.NET Core Kestrel server.
findings.gap: Services exposed directly with no gateway or load balancer. No throttling, no request validation at entry point.
findings.recommendation: Deploy Amazon API Gateway or ALB with AWS WAF for throttling, request validation, and DDoS protection.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configuration
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration exists. Application runs as a single process with no horizontal scaling capability.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling; all capacity is statically provisioned.
findings.recommendation: Configure HPA on EKS based on CPU/memory. Configure Aurora auto-scaling read replicas.

findings.title: No cloud-native backup and recovery
findings.description: Application has built-in local backup but no cloud-native backup infrastructure (no AWS Backup, no PITR, no cross-region replication).
findings.gap: No automated cloud backup with defined retention, no PITR, no cross-region replication.
findings.recommendation: Aurora PostgreSQL includes automated backups with PITR. Configure retention period and cross-region replication.

findings.title: No high availability or multi-AZ deployment
findings.description: Single-instance server design with no multi-AZ deployment. SQLite is a single file; PostgreSQL has no configured failover.
findings.gap: Single-instance deployment with no fault isolation or multi-AZ redundancy.
findings.recommendation: Deploy to EKS across multiple AZs. Use Aurora PostgreSQL Multi-AZ for automatic database failover.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC exists in the repository. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm charts, or Kustomize files found.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage. All infrastructure must be defined from scratch.
findings.recommendation: Define all infrastructure using AWS CDK (C# to leverage team expertise). Start with VPC, EKS, Aurora, ECR, ALB.

findings.title: CI/CD pipeline exists but lacks infrastructure deployment
findings.description: Comprehensive GitHub Actions pipeline with build, test, and release stages. However, no infrastructure deployment automation exists.
findings.gap: CI/CD covers application code but does not deploy infrastructure. No canary or blue/green strategy.
findings.recommendation: Extend pipeline with container image build, ECR push, and IaC deployment stages.

findings.title: Tightly-coupled monolith architecture
findings.description: Application is a single deployable unit with shared database, in-process event bus, and no independent service boundaries.
findings.gap: Tightly-coupled monolith with shared database, in-process communication, and no independently deployable services.
findings.recommendation: Begin with Strangler Fig pattern. Containerize monolith first, then extract services (Notification, Download Client Coordination, Search/Indexer).

findings.title: Background job processing lacks external status tracking and checkpointing
findings.description: Custom in-process command queue with 3 executor threads handles long-running operations. No external status polling, no checkpointing, no crash recovery.
findings.gap: Background job processing exists but with no external status tracking, no checkpointing, no distributed execution.
findings.recommendation: Externalize command queue to AWS Step Functions or SQS. Add idempotency keys and checkpointing.

findings.title: API versioning lacks deprecation timeline
findings.description: Two API versions (V3, V5) coexist with URL-path versioning consistently applied. No documented deprecation timeline for V3.
findings.gap: Versioning exists but no deprecation timeline for V3 and some endpoints are unversioned.
findings.recommendation: Document V3 deprecation timeline. Ensure all new endpoints follow versioning pattern.

findings.title: No dynamic service discovery
findings.description: External service endpoints are user-configured in the database. Update service URL is hardcoded. No dynamic discovery mechanism.
findings.gap: Environment variables for some endpoints but no dynamic discovery.
findings.recommendation: Use Kubernetes DNS-based service discovery when decomposed. Externalize hardcoded endpoints to configuration.

findings.title: Unstructured data on local file system
findings.description: Media metadata and files stored on local/network file systems. No S3 or managed object storage.
findings.gap: Data in local file systems with limited accessibility.
findings.recommendation: Migrate metadata to S3. Use S3 File Gateway for media file access.

findings.title: Mostly centralized data access with some direct SQL
findings.description: Centralized BasicRepository<T> pattern with Dapper ORM. Some raw SQL in specialized repositories for performance-critical queries.
findings.gap: Mostly centralized but some direct Dapper SQL bypasses standard patterns.
findings.recommendation: Existing pattern is strong. Ensure all raw SQL is PostgreSQL-compatible for Aurora migration.

findings.title: Database versions tested but not pinned in IaC
findings.description: PostgreSQL 16/17/18 tested in CI. No version pinning in infrastructure definition (no IaC exists). No documented upgrade procedure.
findings.gap: Versions tested but not pinned. No documented version-update procedure.
findings.recommendation: Pin engine version in IaC when deploying Aurora. Document upgrade procedure.

findings.title: No cloud audit logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail or cloud-native audit logging. Internal auth logging exists but is local, mutable, and not centralized.
findings.gap: No CloudTrail or equivalent cloud audit logging.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail with log file validation and S3 Object Lock. Ship application logs to CloudWatch Logs.

findings.title: No managed encryption at rest
findings.description: No KMS or managed encryption. SQLite is unencrypted on disk. Application-level Data Protection exists for tokens only.
findings.gap: Database storage has no configured encryption at rest.
findings.recommendation: Enable Aurora storage encryption with customer-managed KMS key. Enable SSE-KMS on S3 buckets.

findings.title: Static API key authentication without token-based auth
findings.description: API key authentication via X-Api-Key header. Single shared secret with no per-user attribution, no expiration, no scoped permissions.
findings.gap: API key authentication without OAuth2/JWT. No per-user attribution or token expiration.
findings.recommendation: Integrate Amazon Cognito for OAuth2/JWT. Use API Gateway authorizers for token validation.

findings.title: No centralized identity provider integration
findings.description: Application manages own authentication with local user database. No OIDC, SAML, or OAuth federation. No external IdP integration.
findings.gap: Application manages authentication entirely with no external IdP.
findings.recommendation: Integrate Amazon Cognito as centralized IdP. Implement OIDC for SSO across *arr suite applications.

findings.title: Secrets in local config file without rotation
findings.description: No plaintext credentials in source. Secrets stored in XML config file on disk at runtime without encryption or rotation.
findings.gap: Production credentials in local config file/environment variables without encryption or rotation.
findings.recommendation: Store all secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with automated rotation. Use IAM database authentication for Aurora.

findings.title: No compute hardening or vulnerability scanning
findings.description: No managed patching, no vulnerability scanning, no hardened base images. Legacy Dockerfile uses ubuntu:focal.
findings.gap: No patching strategy, no vulnerability scanning, no hardened images.
findings.recommendation: Use hardened container images (Alpine or Bottlerocket). Enable Amazon Inspector. Expand Dependabot scope.

findings.title: Minimal security scanning in pipeline
findings.description: Dependabot configured but only for devcontainers ecosystem. No SAST, no NuGet/npm vulnerability scanning, no container scanning.
findings.gap: Dependency scanning configured with minimal scope. No SAST or container scanning.
findings.recommendation: Expand Dependabot to nuget and npm. Add CodeQL/Semgrep SAST. Add container image scanning.

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: NLog for structured logging and Sentry for error reporting. No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or trace ID propagation.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing instrumented.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry SDK for .NET with AWS X-Ray exporter.

findings.title: No SLO definitions
findings.description: No SLOs defined. Health checks exist for operational status but no formal service level objectives or error budgets.
findings.gap: No SLOs — no formal definition of acceptable service levels.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for API latency, download tracking completeness, and metadata freshness.

findings.title: No business metrics
findings.description: No custom business metrics published. Sentry captures errors only. Internal statistics visible in UI only.
findings.gap: No custom metrics — only Sentry error reporting.
findings.recommendation: Publish CloudWatch custom metrics for episodes grabbed, import success/failure, indexer response times.

findings.title: No anomaly detection or alerting
findings.description: No anomaly detection or alerting configured. Internal health checks display in UI only, not operational alerts.
findings.gap: No alerting configured.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudWatch alarms for error rates, queue depth, and connection failures.

findings.title: No staged deployment strategy
findings.description: Custom self-update mechanism replaces binaries in-place. No canary, blue/green, or automated rollback.
findings.gap: Direct-to-production with no staged rollout or automated rollback.
findings.recommendation: Implement Kubernetes rolling updates with readiness probes using /ping endpoint. Progress to canary deployments.

findings.title: Integration tests exist with some coverage gaps
findings.description: 18 API test fixtures in CI covering primary workflows. Tests run on 3 platforms and PostgreSQL 16/17/18. Gaps in end-to-end download workflow testing.
findings.gap: Integration tests cover primary workflows but gaps in end-to-end download chain and no contract tests.
findings.recommendation: Add contract tests for external integrations. Add end-to-end smoke tests for download-to-import workflow.

findings.title: No incident response automation
findings.description: No runbooks, no SSM Automation, no self-healing. Internal retry logic exists but no operational incident response.
findings.gap: No runbooks — incident response is ad hoc.
findings.recommendation: Create SSM runbooks for common incidents. Implement Kubernetes pod disruption budgets.

findings.title: No observability ownership
findings.description: No CODEOWNERS for monitoring, no per-service dashboards, no named alarm owners.
findings.gap: No observability ownership — monitoring is reactive and fragmented.
findings.recommendation: Define CODEOWNERS for monitoring. Create per-domain dashboards. Assign alarm ownership.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No AWS resources exist, therefore no tagging. No tagging standard defined.
findings.gap: No tags or tagging governance.
findings.recommendation: Implement tagging standard in IaC from day one (Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter, ManagedBy).

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 (< 3) AND no modern container definitions found (legacy Mono Dockerfiles only)

triggering_questions.note: No managed compute; existing Dockerfiles are legacy Mono-based

detail.container_readiness: Application already supports multiple runtime identifiers, detects Docker environment, has /ping health endpoint

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures). No commercial DB engines detected — uses SQLite and PostgreSQL (both open source).

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures; already open-source databases
Versions tested but not pinned in IaC

detail.migration_complexity: Low — existing Npgsql driver and FluentMigrator are fully compatible with Aurora PostgreSQL

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads, streaming, ETL, or analytics infrastructure exists. Contextual guard: no evidence of data processing workloads.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No streaming, ETL, data pipeline, or analytics artifacts found. The application has no data processing responsibilities beyond its primary CRUD operations.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=1 (< 3, no IaC), OPS-Q5=1 (< 3, no staged deployment)

triggering_questions.note: In-place binary replacement, no staged rollout

immediate_actions: Add container build stage to CI

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context describes 'TV-series PVR for usenet and BitTorrent users' with no AI-related signal terms.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.
